title: USGS Minerals Yearbook — The Mineral Industry of Argentina (Volume III, Area Reports: International), comparator to Iran coverage
source_org: U.S. Geological Survey, National Minerals Information Center
download_url: https://pubs.usgs.gov/myb/vol3/{year}/myb3-{year}-argentina.pdf (and .xlsx) — see files list for exact per-year URLs

files.name: myb3-2020-21-Argentina-ert.xlsx
files.type: tables-only advance-release (XLSX, no full narrative PDF published)

frequency: annual (some years combined into biennial reports)
units: metric tons / other units as described per-table in each file
notes: Comparator dataset to usgs-minerals-yearbook/irn/, and directly complementary to this project's existing Argentina fleet (indec-argentina, bcra-argentina, argentina-ministry-economy, argentina-inflation-reconstruction — see SOURCES.md Round 26). Argentina's USGS coverage is the thinnest of the 5 comparators: no full narrative PDF report after 2019, and only a 2020-21 tables-only advance release exists after that — no 2022 or 2023 data of any kind found on the official page (https://www.usgs.gov/centers/national-minerals-information-center/south-america#arg). Argentina is notably home to the world's 3rd-largest lithium reserves per USGS overview text, making the 2016-2019 lithium exploration-budget tables in these files particularly relevant. All files verified genuine PDF/Excel via `file`.

failures.reason: not published by USGS — absent from official country page link list; most recent available data is the 2020-21 tables-only advance release
